title: MarginalizationPreserves

lean_statement.name: marginalize_anc_iff
lean_statement.code:
/-- claim_3_16 part 1/4 (LN remark item 1): for any CDMG `G` and any
set `W`, marginalization preserves ancestral relations on vertices
outside `W`. For `v₁, v₂ ∉ W`, `v₁` is an ancestor of `v₂` in `G` iff
`v₁` is an ancestor of `v₂` in the marginalization `G.marginalize W`.

The implicit `v₁ ∈ G` precondition of the LN's "$v_1, v_2 \in G$"
preamble is already carried by the `mem_Anc` membership on each side
(both `G.Anc v₂` and `(G.marginalize W).Anc v₂` are set-builders
guarded by `_ ∈ G` resp. `_ ∈ G.marginalize W`); the explicit
`v_i ∉ W` hypotheses bridge the two memberships. -/
theorem marginalize_anc_iff (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α) {v₁ v₂ : α}
    (h₁ : v₁ ∉ W) (h₂ : v₂ ∉ W) :
    v₁ ∈ G.Anc v₂ ↔ v₁ ∈ (G.marginalize W).Anc v₂ := by

lean_statement.name: marginalize_bifurcation_iff
lean_statement.code:
/-- claim_3_16 part 2/4 (LN remark item 2, no-source half): for any
CDMG `G` and any set `W`, marginalization preserves the existence of
bifurcations between two vertices `v₁, v₂ ∈ G \ W`. The LN's "between"
is read symmetrically in `v₁, v₂` and encoded as a disjunction over
the two walk directions; the disjunction shape is also what makes the
biconditional handle the `L^{\sm W}` exclusion clause cleanly (a bidir
hinge in `G` absorbed by the exclusion still gives a `.backward`-hinge
bifurcation in `G^{\sm W}` *in the opposite walk direction*).

The with-source variant ("with source `v_3`") is intentionally
*deferred* to a follow-up dispatch. -/
theorem marginalize_bifurcation_iff (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α)
    {v₁ v₂ : α} (h₁v : v₁ ∈ G) (h₂v : v₂ ∈ G)
    (h₁W : v₁ ∉ W) (h₂W : v₂ ∉ W) :
    ((∃ π : Walk G v₁ v₂, π.IsBifurcation) ∨
     (∃ π : Walk G v₂ v₁, π.IsBifurcation)) ↔
    ((∃ π : Walk (G.marginalize W) v₁ v₂, π.IsBifurcation) ∨
     (∃ π : Walk (G.marginalize W) v₂ v₁, π.IsBifurcation)) := by

lean_statement.name: marginalize_isAcyclic
lean_statement.code:
/-- claim_3_16 part 3/4 (LN remark item 3a): for any CDMG `G` and any
set `W`, if `G` is acyclic then so is the marginalization
`G.marginalize W`.

The mathematical content is that every directed edge of `G.marginalize
W` shortcuts a length-`≥ 1` directed walk through `W` in `G` (by
`mem_marginalize_E`), so a non-trivial directed cycle in
`G.marginalize W` expands to a non-trivial directed cycle in `G`,
contradicting `G.IsAcyclic`. This route is independent of claim_3_2
(no topological-order extraction); the topological-order
preservation half is the sibling theorem
`marginalize_isTopologicalOrder` below. -/
theorem marginalize_isAcyclic (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α)
    (h : G.IsAcyclic) : (G.marginalize W).IsAcyclic := by

lean_statement.name: marginalize_isTopologicalOrder
lean_statement.code:
/-- claim_3_16 part 4/4 (LN remark item 3b): for any CDMG `G` and any
set `W`, a topological order `r` of `G` is also a topological order of
the marginalization `G.marginalize W`. The same relation `r` carries
through — the LN's "by just ignoring the nodes from `W`" — because
`IsTopologicalOrder` quantifies its fields over the (smaller) vertex
set of `G.marginalize W`.

The four `IsTopologicalOrder` fields (irreflexivity, transitivity,
trichotomy, parent-precedence) all reduce field-by-field to the
corresponding statement on `G`: the first three by restriction along
`v ∈ G.marginalize W → v ∈ G`, the fourth by chaining `r` along the
directed walk through `W` that underlies each `G.marginalize W` edge
(via `mem_marginalize_E`). -/
theorem marginalize_isTopologicalOrder (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α)
    {r : α → α → Prop} (hr : G.IsTopologicalOrder r) :
    (G.marginalize W).IsTopologicalOrder r := by

lean_explanation:
The LN remark bundles three structural preservation properties of marginalization into one block; the Lean encoding splits this into four theorems, one per logically independent sub-claim. Throughout, `α : Type*` is the ambient vertex type, `G : CDMG α` is the CDMG $G = (J, V, E, L)$, and `W : Set α` is the set of nodes being marginalised out (the LN's $W$); `G.marginalize W` is the result $G^{\sm W}$. No hypothesis `W ⊆ G.V` is required — `marginalize` accepts any `W : Set α`, and the LN's set-relative reading is recovered at each use site.

In `marginalize_anc_iff`, `G.Anc v₂` is the ancestor set $\Anc^G(v_2)$ — a `Set α` whose membership predicate carries `_ ∈ G` and the existence of a length-`≥ 0` directed walk. Because `_ ∈ G` is already syntactically embedded in the set-builder on each side, the LN's preamble "$v_1, v_2 \in G$" needs no explicit hypothesis here; only the `v_i ∉ W` clauses are quoted.

In `marginalize_bifurcation_iff`, `Walk G v₁ v₂` is the type of walks from `v₁` to `v₂` (direction-aware), and `π.IsBifurcation` says $\pi$ has the LN's $\hut\cdots\hut\hus\tuh\cdots\tuh$ V-shape. The LN's symmetric word "between $v_1, v_2$" is unpacked as the disjunction `(∃ π : Walk … v₁ v₂, π.IsBifurcation) ∨ (∃ π : Walk … v₂ v₁, π.IsBifurcation)` on both sides of the iff — `Walk.IsBifurcation` is not symmetric across reversal in our encoding, so the two walk directions have to be carried explicitly. The four hypotheses `v_i ∈ G` and `v_i ∉ W` together unfold the LN's `v_i ∈ G \ W`.

In `marginalize_isAcyclic`, `G.IsAcyclic` is the field-level acyclicity predicate ("no length-`≥ 1` directed cycle through any `v ∈ G`"); the theorem transports this property from $G$ to $G^{\sm W}$ without any vertex hypothesis. In `marginalize_isTopologicalOrder`, `r : α → α → Prop` is a binary relation on the ambient type (implicit because unifiable from `hr`), and `G.IsTopologicalOrder r` bundles four axioms — irreflexivity, transitivity, trichotomy, parent-precedence — each quantified over `v ∈ G`. The conclusion `(G.marginalize W).IsTopologicalOrder r` says the *same* relation `r`, restricted to the smaller vertex set of $G^{\sm W}$, still satisfies those four axioms — the LN's "by just ignoring the nodes from $W$".
design_choices:
**Four theorems instead of one bundled `Prop`.** The LN remark packs three preservation properties (ancestors, bifurcations, acyclicity-and-topological-order) into a single block, but downstream consumers want them separately: claim_3_17 cites items 1 + 2, claim_3_18 / 3_19 and chapter 4 cite item 3a, chapter 5's do-calculus cites item 3b. A bundled `Prop` would force every consumer to destructure or to chain irrelevant hypotheses. Item 3 is further split into 3a (acyclicity) and 3b (topological order) because the two are logically independent: 3a is provable directly by walk concatenation through `mem_marginalize_E`, without going through claim_3_2's "acyclic iff has a topological order" equivalence; 3b carries the *same* relation `r` rather than re-extracting an order via `Classical.choice`.

**Item 2 split into no-source and with-source variants; only no-source landed here.** The LN's parenthetical "(with source $v_3$)" interacts non-trivially with the `L^{\sm W}` exclusion clause from `mem_marginalize_L`: when a bidirected hinge of a bifurcation in $G$ is absorbed by the exclusion, the resulting bifurcation in $G^{\sm W}$ is forced to use a backward hinge in the *opposite* walk direction, manufacturing a source where the LN bifurcation had none. The no-source biconditional absorbs this cleanly via the symmetric-`∨` reading; the with-source biconditional does not, and is deferred to a follow-up dispatch that will introduce a helper predicate `Walk.IsBifurcationWithSource` in the right place.

**Symmetric `∨` reading of "between".** The LN's word "between" is symmetric in $v_1, v_2$, but `Walk G v w` is directional and `Walk.IsBifurcation` is not symmetric across reversal in our encoding (the `leftArm` / `rightArm` split is direction-aware). The clean Lean rendering of "a bifurcation exists between $v_1$ and $v_2$" is therefore the disjunction over the two walk directions, used on both sides of `marginalize_bifurcation_iff`. The same convention is what lets the biconditional handle the `L^{\sm W}` exclusion-clause case-split cleanly.

**Asymmetric preconditions across the four theorems.** `marginalize_anc_iff` takes only `v_i ∉ W` because `_ ∈ G` is already embedded in the `Anc` set-builder on each side; `marginalize_bifurcation_iff` takes both `v_i ∈ G` and `v_i ∉ W` because `IsBifurcation`'s existential does not embed `v_i ∈ G` syntactically; `marginalize_isAcyclic` and `marginalize_isTopologicalOrder` take no vertex hypothesis because `IsAcyclic` / `IsTopologicalOrder` are themselves `∀ v ∈ G, ...` statements. We use `v_i ∉ W` rather than `v_i ∈ G.marginalize W`: the latter would be strictly weaker when `W ∩ G.J ≠ ∅` (input nodes survive marginalisation regardless of `W`-membership), and the LN literally writes "$\notin W$".

**`marginalize_*` prefix throughout, deviating from the project's `<conclusion>_<construction>` convention.** The sibling rows use names like `isAcyclic_nodeSplittingOn`, `isTopologicalOrder_nodeSplittingOn`. Here we use `marginalize_anc_iff` / `marginalize_bifurcation_iff` / `marginalize_isAcyclic` / `marginalize_isTopologicalOrder` because this file studies a single operation — marginalization — and the four theorems are unified by what they study, not by what each concludes. The shared prefix matches `Marginalization.lean`'s own projection names (`marginalize_J`, `marginalize_V`, `mem_marginalize_E`, `mem_marginalize_L`) and reads cleanly under dot-notation at call sites (`G.marginalize_isAcyclic W h`).
